sanskrit:
ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्नुते।
अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते॥
transliteration: jñeyaṃ yat tat pravakṣyāmi yaj jñātvāmṛtam aśnute | anādi mat-paraṃ brahma na sat tan nāsad ucyate ||
literal_meaning: I shall proclaim that which is to be known, having known which one tastes immortality; beginningless, supreme, related to Me, called Brahman—neither being nor non-being it is said.
contextual_meaning: Krishna points to a field of awareness that precedes every story the mind spins about itself—an unborn, deathless knowingness that can be tasted here-now, dissolving the fear of time and insignificance.
psychological_state: A moment when the usual objects of knowledge (roles, achievements, past, future) lose their grip and the client senses a background awareness that has never been harmed.
trigger_scenario: A successful executive on sabbatical stares at the ocean and suddenly cannot find the 'I' who is supposed to be relaxing; instead of panic, there is a sweet taste of 'am-ness' without content.
anchor_text: Rest as the aware silence before every thought.
